title: Phase 5: Derive --min-year dynamically instead of hardcoding 2026
description: update-feed.yml line 95 hardcodes --min-year 2026 for generate-feed.ts. The moment April 2027 conference data is scraped, the feed will still be filtered to 2026+ which is fine, but the real risk is the reverse: if someone changes intent (latest N years), there's no dynamic fallback. More importantly: once 2026 content is archival, downstream consumers may want auto-rolling windows. Add an env var or derive from current date so the feed window is self-maintaining.

title: Phase 5: Add concurrency control to update-feed workflow
description: .github/workflows/update-feed.yml has no `concurrency:` block. Scheduled cron runs during conference windows (3x daily) can overlap with workflow_dispatch manual runs; both jobs commit to main and push, racing the output/ JSON files and docs/*.xml. Add concurrency with group per branch and cancel-in-progress=false so runs serialize rather than drop.

title: Phase 4: Legal/TOS review for hotlinking or caching church imagery
description: Review churchofjesuschrist.org terms of service for image reuse in a third-party podcast feed. Compare hotlinking (no copy) vs caching in repo vs external CDN. Must complete before any pipeline work ships publicly.
close_reason: Resolved: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints has a personal-use exception that covers this project's use cases (personal podcast feed generation from publicly available audio/imagery). User confirmed 2026-04-20. Phase 4 per-episode artwork track is unblocked.

title: Phase 1: Fail loud when all parser fallbacks return empty sessions
description: src/scraper.ts extractSessionsViaDocMap / ViaDataContentType / ViaClassNames silently return [] on no match, masking HTML contract changes. Add circuit breaker: if all three return 0 sessions on a known-good conference URL, throw rather than write an empty JSON.

title: Phase 1: Atomic writes for output JSON (temp then rename)
description: src/scrape-all.ts overwrites JSON files directly. Partial scrape produces corrupted/truncated file, destroying the previous complete snapshot. Write to <file>.tmp then atomic rename.

title: Phase 1: Exponential backoff + retry in fetchWithRateLimit
description: src/scraper.ts fetchWithRateLimit does a single attempt and dies on transient 429/503/timeout. Add 3 retries with exponential backoff and jitter. Surface permanent failures to the caller with context.

title: Phase 1: Runtime schema validation on scraped API responses and output JSON
description: src/scraper.ts JSON.parse calls assume ApiResponse/ConferenceOutput shapes without runtime validation. Add zod schemas and validate at API ingest and JSON deserialization. Fail loud on contract drift instead of silently producing empty/partial output.

title: Phase 5: Skip feed regeneration for languages whose scrape failed under continue-on-error
description: update-feed.yml marks Spanish/Portuguese scrapes as continue-on-error: true. When they fail, the next step still runs generate-feed.ts --all-languages, regenerating audio-es.xml / audio-pt.xml from the last-known (possibly stale) JSON. Capture per-language scrape success and skip writing the corresponding feed when the scrape failed, so subscribers are not silently served stale data without even a new timestamp.

title: Phase 5: Emit GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY with scrape metrics
description: Scrape step output (scraped/skipped/failed counts, per-language status, duration) is currently buried in raw workflow logs. Teach scrape-all.ts or a wrapper step to append a markdown summary to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY so operators can see at-a-glance health on the Actions run page. Complements Phase 0 structured logging.

title: Phase 5: Validate workflow_dispatch inputs (languages, start_year, force_scrape combinations)
description: update-feed.yml workflow_dispatch accepts `languages` as a free string; inputs like `jpn` match no branch, silently skipping spa/por while still running English. `start_year` is not numeric-validated. `start_year` silently overrides `force_scrape=true` via the if/elif chain in the English scrape step. Add an input-validation step at job start that fails early with a helpful message.

title: Phase 5: Add timeout-minutes to all workflow jobs
description: update-feed and deploy jobs use the default 6-hour timeout. A hung network call in scrape-all.ts ties up a runner for hours before alerting. Set update-feed timeout-minutes to ~30, deploy to ~10. Calibrate from observed normal runtimes.

title: Epic: Future-proofing code review initiative (umbrella)
description: Tracks the six-phase initiative from the exhaustive future-proofing code review. Phase 0 foundation (tests/lint/types/logging), Phase 1 critical reliability (schema validation, retries, atomic writes, CI alerts), Phase 2 scraper future-proofing, Phase 3 RSS/Podcast 2.0 standards, Phase 4 per-episode artwork (investigation then implementation), Phase 5 CI/build hardening, Phase 6 docs/versioning. See sibling Phase-N issues for concrete work.

title: Phase 5: Fix deploy job race condition with ff-only pull
description: deploy job git pull between update and deploy can pick up conflicting commits silently. Use checkout@v4 with fetch-depth and run git pull --ff-only; fail explicitly if not fast-forwardable.

title: Phase 4: Decide image delivery strategy (hotlink vs git-lfs vs external CDN)
description: Given TOS findings and Apple size requirements, pick a strategy. Record decision and tradeoffs in bd --design and link back to SPEC.md.
design: DECISION (2026-04-20): TIERED APPROACH - Start with hotlinking, cache locally only if needed. ## Chosen strategy: HOTLINK to church IIIF CDN (Phase 4a) The URL on each feed item points directly to the church's own image CDN: https://www.churchofjesuschrist.org/imgs//full/!1400%2C1400/0/default.jpg ## Rationale 1. LEGAL CLEAR: Church has personal-use exception covering this project (gc_podcast-9y6). 2. CONTENT-ADDRESSED: Image URLs use opaque hashes that are stable per-revision - podcast client caches will stay valid indefinitely. 3. IIIF SIZE CONTROL: URL path encodes target dimensions; we can request any size (incl. 1400x1400 for Apple Podcasts compliance) without any local image processing. 4. ZERO STORAGE COST: No repo bloat, no LFS quotas, no CI upload pipeline, no external object storage account. 5. SIMPLEST SHIP: Implementation is just regex extraction + URL templating; existing scraper/cache handles fetch. 6. LOW RISK: If church ever blocks hot-linking or changes CDN, we learn quickly (podcast clients will 404) and pivot to Phase 4b. ## Tradeoffs considered - GIT-LFS: rejected - GitHub free tier is 1GB storage + 1GB/month bandwidth; ~80MB rolling 2-yr window would fit but operational complexity is high (slow clones, LFS server dependency, CI credentials). - GITHUB PAGES STATIC (no LFS): feasible (~80MB). Rejected for Phase 4a because hotlink is simpler and the church's IIIF CDN is already a stable, purpose-built image server. Keep as Phase 4b fallback if needed. - EXTERNAL CDN (R2/B2): rejected - adds operational overhead (account, creds, billing) for marginal benefit on a FOSS project. ## Apple Podcasts compliance note Apple requires >=1400px square JPEG. The church IIIF server can deliver !1400%2C1400 (best-fit within box) but source portraits are 4:5 aspect, meaning a !1400%2C1400 request returns ~1120x1400 (letterboxed/padded is not applied by IIIF by default). IF Apple strictness becomes a real problem: - Option A: request /square/1400,1400/0/default.jpg (IIIF region crop - center-crop) - Option B: switch to Phase 4b (local download + pad/crop + commit) Many podcast clients (Overcast, Pocket Casts, Castro, Apple Podcasts in practice) accept non-square per-episode artwork. Start with !1400, and only square-crop if real clients reject. ## Per-item source priority (locked in from spikes db3 + lfk) 1. Talk page og:image (primary - most modern talks have it) 2. Talk page __INITIAL_STATE__ JSON 'image' field (fallback when og absent) 3. First hero in talk body (last-resort body scrape) 4. Speaker bio portrait (/learn/ page - fallback for talks with no hero imagery) 5. Channel default artwork (final fallback) All sources converge on the same IIIF URL format: /imgs//full//0/default. Extractor is a single regex. ## Downstream impact on blocked work - gc_podcast-0k8 'Image pipeline - download/transcode/host': NARROW to just URL normalization (extract hash, template to 1400px). No download, no transcode, no host needed for Phase 4a. Can be absorbed into gc_podcast-mnu. - gc_podcast-mnu 'Extend scraper to fetch bio portrait': EXPAND scope to talk-page-first, bio-as-fallback extraction. - gc_podcast-dt6 'Add image_url field': ALREADY DONE via gc_podcast-c7v infrastructure. - gc_podcast-49k 'Wire per-item ': ALREADY DONE via gc_podcast-c7v. ## Rollback path if hotlinking breaks If church CDN blocks hotlink referers OR podcast clients report 403s OR legal position changes: 1. Pivot to Phase 4b: download during scrape, normalize to 1400x1400 JPEG, commit to docs/images/-/.jpg, URL feed to github.io. 2. Existing extraction logic stays; only the 'how to host' layer changes.
close_reason: Strategy decided: HOTLINK to church IIIF CDN for Phase 4a. Per-item <itunes:image> URLs point to /imgs/<hash>/full/!1400%2C1400/0/default.jpg. Rationale recorded in --design. Phase 4b (local cache + normalize) reserved as fallback. This narrows gc_podcast-0k8 (no download/transcode needed), expands gc_podcast-mnu (talk-page-first extraction).

title: Phase 5: Bump Node engines to >=20 and align CI
description: package.json engines says Node >=18 (EOL April 2024) but CI uses setup-node@v4 with Node 20. Pin engines to >=20.0.0,<22.0.0 to match the Node 20 LTS window.

title: Phase 5: Remove or unify orphaned build/dist pipeline
description: npm run build compiles TS to dist/ but nothing consumes it — dev/scrape/feed/scrape-all all run tsx directly, and CI never calls build. Either delete the build script and main field, or refactor CI and runtime to execute from dist. Current hybrid is cargo culted.

title: Phase 1: Fix CI cache key - currently unique per run and never reused
description: Cache key uses github.run_id (line 64 of update-feed.yml), making every run a cache miss. Change to hashFiles('package-lock.json') with restore-keys fallback.

title: Phase 4: Investigate church speaker bio page image availability and format
description: Spike (~4 hours). Pick 5 representative speakers (apostle, seventy, woman leader, foreign-language variant, historical). Fetch bio page HTML, locate portrait URL (og:image and/or in-page img), record dimensions/size/CDN host. Document findings in docs/ or bd notes.
notes: Spike findings (2026-04-20): SAMPLES: 5 speakers across required categories - Dallin H. Oaks (Q12): /learn/dallin-h-oaks - og:image YES (1600w) - Jeffrey R. Holland (Q12): /learn/jeffrey-r-holland - og:image NO, body img only - Carlos A. Godoy (Seventy): /learn/carlos-a-godoy - og:image NO, body img only - Camille N. Johnson (RS Gen Pres): /learn/camille-n-johnson - og:image YES; same asset returned for lang=eng and lang=spa - Brook P. Hales (Seventy, 2018): /learn/brook-p-hales - og:image NO, body img only - Richard G. Scott (deceased): 404 - deceased speakers do NOT have /learn/ pages URL PATTERN: /learn/--?lang= - matches existing constructBioUrl() at src/scraper.ts:1262. STABLE. CDN: IIIF Image API 2.1 on churchofjesuschrist.org/imgs//full//0/default - Asset IDs are content-hash-like (40 hex or opaque token); immutable per revision - Format: JPEG (append /default.jpg to force) - Size-controllable via URL: full/1400,/ or full/!1400%2C1400/ - Portraits are language-agnostic (same asset across eng/spa) - No auth/paywall KEY FINDING: og:image NOT universal - only 2 of 5 had it. Body fallback required for ~60% of speakers. RECOMMENDATION (for gc_podcast-mnu): 1. Reuse constructBioUrl() + fetchWithRateLimit 2. Handle 404 gracefully (deceased speakers) 3. Extract via regex: /churchofjesuschrist\.org\/imgs\/([a-z0-9]+)\/full\//i - works for both og:image AND body imgs 4. Prefer og:image, fall back to first body match, return undefined on miss 5. Rebuild canonical URL at target size: /full/,1400/0/default.jpg (aspect-preserved) or /full/!1400%2C1400/0/default.jpg (square-fit for Apple Podcasts) 6. Dedup: Map> - one fetch per speaker per run 7. Populate talk.speaker.image_url; rss-generator already emits RISKS: - og:image not universal (primary risk) - need robust body-img fallback - Slug guessing brittle for edge cases (Jr, accents, non-Western names) - add override map - Historical speakers (pre-2010) often 404 - acceptable, falls back to channel image - Aspect ratio: portraits are 4:5, Apple wants square - center-crop or pad if Apple compat matters - Re-scrape periodically (6mo) to pick up new portraits FEASIBILITY: CONFIRMED - straightforward automation.
close_reason: Spike complete. Extraction is feasible via IIIF URL pattern. og:image NOT universal (only 2 of 5 samples); robust body-img fallback required via regex /churchofjesuschrist\.org\/imgs\/([a-z0-9]+)\/full\//i. Deceased speakers 404 - acceptable fallback to channel image. Findings captured in issue notes; ready for gc_podcast-mnu implementation.

title: Phase 1: CI failure alerting - open GitHub issue on workflow failure
description: .github/workflows/update-feed.yml runs 3x daily during conference windows with no alerting. Add 'if: failure()' step that opens an issue or posts to a notification channel so outages during Apr/Oct release windows are noticed.

title: Phase 2: Detect partial sessions (missing audio, not just empty talks array)
description: src/scrape-all.ts isIncomplete() only checks session.talks.length===0. Extend to detect sessions with talks missing audio URLs or missing duration, so the retry-incomplete loop actually catches them.

title: Phase 1: Rich error context in scrape failure logs
description: Failures currently log 'Failed to fetch talk details for X' with no conference/session/speaker context. Wrap errors with structured context so CI logs are actionable.

title: Phase 3: Add Podcasting 2.0 namespace and <podcast:guid>
description: src/rss-generator.ts RSS root declares itunes/content/atom namespaces but not podcast 2.0. Add xmlns:podcast="https://podcastindex.org/namespace/1.0" and include a stable channel-level <podcast:guid> for feed portability and modern client support.

title: Phase 2: Monitor API response schema and warn on drift
description: churchofjesuschrist.org API-first discovery (commit 969332a) assumes meta.title / meta.audio[] / content.body stay stable. Add contract assertions that warn when shape drifts without failing hard; pairs with Phase 1 zod validation.

title: Phase 2: Extract language codes and URL/audio mappings to config
description: src/scraper.ts hardcodes eng/spa/por in LANG_URL_MAP / LANG_AUDIO_MAP and .github/workflows/update-feed.yml hardcodes matrix cases. Move to a single languages.ts config so adding a new language is one edit.

title: Phase 0: Set up test infrastructure with HTML fixtures
description: Add vitest, create tests/fixtures with snapshots of conference HTML (doc-map, data-content-type, class-based variants). Provides foundation for safely refactoring scraper/RSS generator.
notes: Capture HTML from 2026-04 (modern), 2018-04 (transitional), and one pre-2015 page to cover all three parser fallbacks in src/scraper.ts.

title: Phase 4c: Discovery - semi-annual conference-branded channel artwork
description:
Research spike. User observation (2026-04-20): the Church publishes a full PDF/magazine issue with each General Conference (November issue = October conference, May issue = April conference) containing conference-specific artwork and photography. Session-specific artwork may also be available on the website.

Hypothesis: the feed's channel-level <itunes:image> (currently a static project-default) could rotate every 6 months to match the latest conference's branding, giving the podcast a fresh visual identity at each release cycle.

This is DISTINCT from gc_podcast-mnu (per-item artwork on each episode/talk). This is channel-level artwork that changes twice a year.

## Discovery questions

1. Does churchofjesuschrist.org publish a conference-branded hero image per session? URL patterns to investigate:
   - /study/general-conference/<year>/<mm>?lang=eng - landing page for a given conference
   - og:image on that page - is it conference-specific or generic?
   - Any session-specific imagery (Sat AM, Sat PM, Sun AM, Sun PM, Priesthood, Women's)?
2. Does the Liahona / Ensign / Friend magazine (May and November issues) have a downloadable cover image URL? What format/dimensions?
3. Is there a 'conference report' or 'conference art' landing page that curates each conference's visuals?
4. Do the church's YouTube/BYU TV feeds publish conference-branded thumbnails we can reference?
5. Apple Podcasts requires channel artwork 1400-3000px square JPEG/PNG - do any identified sources meet this?

## Potential implementation (if research confirms feasibility)

- Add a conference-to-artwork lookup (manual mapping or scraped)
- When generating feeds, pick artwork for the most recent conference in the feed window
- Emit as channel-level <itunes:image> and <image><url>
- Regenerate on each scrape (post-conference auto-refresh)

## Tradeoffs to explore

- Pro: Fresh visual identity aligned with each release - matches user's intuition
- Pro: Ties podcast to official church branding per cycle
- Con: Channel artwork change may confuse podcast clients (some cache channel image aggressively)
- Con: Some users prefer stable channel identity; per-item artwork (mnu) already gives per-episode freshness
- Neutral: Legal cleared under personal-use exception same as per-item artwork

## Deliverable

Findings report documenting:
- Whether conference-branded artwork is reliably discoverable
- Source URLs and extraction patterns
- Dimension/format suitability for Apple Podcasts channel artwork
- Recommendation: implement / defer / reject with rationale
- If implement: file follow-up implementation issue with concrete plan

User noted 'more Discovery and research is needed' - this is research-only, no implementation in this issue.

title: Phase 4b: Use /learn/global-leadership-of-the-church as authoritative speaker-to-bio-URL index
description: Enhancement to gc_podcast-mnu bio-fallback path. Currently (per mnu implementation) the bio URL is constructed by slugifying the speaker's name (constructBioUrl()). This is brittle for edge cases (Jr, accents, non-Western name orders, renames) - risk documented in gc_podcast-db3 findings. ## Alternative: scrape the directory index https://www.churchofjesuschrist.org/learn/global-leadership-of-the-church?lang=eng This page lists every current General Authority with a link to their /learn/ bio page. Scraping it once per run gives an authoritative {displayName -> bioUrl} map, eliminating slug-guessing entirely for current GAs. Confirmed sample URLs from that directory: - https://www.churchofjesuschrist.org/learn/kevin-g-brown?lang=eng - https://www.churchofjesuschrist.org/learn/kristin-m-yee?lang=eng ## Design 1. Add getSpeakerBioIndex(lang): Promise> to scraper 2. Fetch /learn/global-leadership-of-the-church once per scrape-run (memoize) 3. Parse anchor tags: Display Name 4. Build a normalized-name -> full-URL map 5. In the bio-fallback path: try map[speaker.name] first, fall back to constructBioUrl() if not found (preserves support for historical / non-GA speakers like musical presenters) ## Tradeoffs - Pro: Eliminates slug-guessing risk for current GAs (majority of speakers) - Pro: Catches renames/re-slugs automatically - Pro: Small additional cost - one fetch per run per language, cached - Con: Page structure could change; need graceful fallback - Con: Only covers current GAs (not emeritus, deceased, guest musical presenters) ## Acceptance criteria - Directory fetched at most once per run per language - Normalized name matching (case-insensitive, diacritic-folded) - Falls through to existing constructBioUrl() slugify when name not in index - Unit tests against a captured fixture of the index page - Integration test: known-good speaker name resolves to correct /learn URL via index ## Blocks None - this is a hardening enhancement on top of gc_podcast-mnu. Safe to defer until mnu ships and we see real-world slug-failure rates.

title: Phase 0 followup: husky pre-commit hook missing shebang, fails on Windows git exec
description: The .husky/pre-commit file created in gc_podcast-aay has no shebang line, so Git on Windows cannot exec it directly (Exec format error). Agents have had to bypass with HUSKY=0 git commit. Add a #!/usr/bin/env sh shebang or rewrite to use husky v9's native mechanism.
close_reason: Added shebang to .husky/pre-commit; verified hook now executes on Windows git.

title: Phase 0 followup: Normalize formatting with one-time Prettier sweep
description: ESLint and husky landed in gc_podcast-aay but Prettier format:check was deferred because src/ has CRLF/LF line ending mix, aligned trailing-comment blocks in types.ts, and multi-line layouts that Prettier collapses. Run npm run format once to normalize, verify scraper/RSS output is unchanged, then add format:check to the CI lint gate (gc_podcast-6rp).

title: Phase 5: Pin runner OS to ubuntu-24.04 instead of ubuntu-latest
description: Both jobs in update-feed.yml use runs-on: ubuntu-latest, which is a moving target that changes without notice when GitHub rolls the default. Pin to ubuntu-24.04 for reproducibility; upgrade explicitly when ready.

title: Phase 6: Add SPEC.md changelog and enforce schema version at runtime
description: SPEC.md has no changelog section; JSON output version:1.0 is never validated at read time. Add a Changelog section and a runtime version guard in generate-feed.ts and scrape-all.ts.

title: Phase 6: Adopt semver and wire version into RSS <generator>
description: package.json is frozen at 1.0.0. Adopt semver, bump on schema or feed-contract changes, and emit <generator>gc-podcast-scraper/X.Y.Z</generator> in the RSS output so subscribers can see which version produced the feed.

title: Phase 6: Auto-generate docs/index.html from feed metadata
description: docs/index.html is hand-maintained and will go stale (hardcoded 'April 2026 currently available'). Generate it from a small template + output/feeds list so adding a language or conference just updates one data source.

title: Phase 4: Wire per-item <itunes:image> emission in RSS using populated image_url
description: Once Phase 3 infrastructure is in and Phase 4 scraper populates image_url, enable per-item image emission in feeds. Add integration test verifying feed XML contains <itunes:image> inside <item>.

title: Phase 4: Image pipeline - download, transcode to Apple-compliant JPEG, host
description: If decision (issue 4) is to cache images rather than hotlink, build a pipeline that fetches source images, transcodes to <=512KB square 1400-3000px JPEG, and publishes them via GitHub Pages under docs/images/. Keep stable filenames keyed off speaker slug.
close_reason: Obsoleted by gbt decision to hotlink church IIIF CDN. No download, no transcode, no GitHub Pages hosting needed for Phase 4a. URL normalization logic (hash extraction + !1400%2C1400 templating) absorbs into gc_podcast-mnu. Reserved as Phase 4b fallback if hotlinking proves unreliable.

title: Phase 5: Add retry with backoff to scrape step (not just continue-on-error)
description: Spanish/Portuguese scrape steps have continue-on-error: true, English has no safety net. Add GH Actions retry with exponential backoff so transient failures self-heal instead of shipping partial feeds.

title: Phase 4: Extract per-talk image URL from talk pages (primary) and speaker bio (fallback)
description: Extend scraper to populate image_url on each Talk (primary source: talk page imagery) and Speaker (fallback: /learn/ bio portrait). Based on spike findings db3+lfk and delivery decision gbt. ## Extraction source priority (per talk) 1. Talk page (primary - most modern talks) 2. Talk page window.__INITIAL_STATE__ JSON 'image' field (fallback when og missing; common) 3. First hero in talk article body (last-resort body scrape) 4. Speaker bio portrait from /learn/ page (fallback for talks with no hero) 5. Leave unset - channel-level is the final fallback ## URL normalization All church imagery is IIIF: /imgs//full//0/default Extract hash via regex: /churchofjesuschrist\.org\/imgs\/([a-z0-9]+)\/full\//i Rebuild canonical URL at podcast size: /imgs//full/!1400%2C1400/0/default.jpg Handle double-encoded inner URLs (/imgs/https%3A%2F%2F...%2Fimgs%2F...) - unwrap before extracting. ## Implementation notes - Reuse fetchWithRateLimit + existing cache layer (no new HTTP infra) - Dedup bio fetches: Map> so each speaker hits /learn/ at most once per run - Handle 404s gracefully (deceased speakers, missing bio pages) - Slug override map for edge cases (Jr, accents, non-Western names) - No download/transcode needed (hotlink strategy per gbt) ## Test requirements - Unit: regex extracts from og:image, __INITIAL_STATE__, body img, and double-encoded forms - Unit: URL normalization produces canonical !1400%2C1400 form - Integration: sample talk JSON round-trips with image_url populated - Fixture: real talk page HTML (modern + historical) checked into tests/fixtures/

title: Phase 4: Add image_url field to Speaker and Talk types
description: Extend src/types.ts Speaker and optionally Talk with image_url (nullable). Bump schema version. Update SPEC.md section on data format.
close_reason: Already done via gc_podcast-c7v infrastructure: src/types.ts has optional image_url on Speaker, Talk, Session (3 locations). src/schemas.ts has matching zod schemas. SPEC.md doc update can be folded into a separate docs pass (see gc_podcast-hjq Phase 6 SPEC changelog) rather than held as a separate task.

title: Phase 2: Clarify temporal speaker-role semantics (role-at-time-of-talk vs role-now)
description: classifyRole() in src/scraper.ts infers role from author-role text on the current page, which can drift when the church updates historical pages. Decide whether to freeze role at first-scrape or always re-derive, document the choice in SPEC.md, and implement accordingly.

title: Phase 5: Pin GitHub Actions to commit SHAs and enable Dependabot
description: checkout, setup-node, cache, configure-pages, upload/deploy-pages all reference mutable @v4 tags. Pin to SHAs and enable Dependabot to auto-PR upgrades so supply-chain drift is reviewed.
close_reason: Pinned all GitHub Actions to explicit SHAs with version tags and created dependabot config for weekly updates

title: Phase 2: Runtime schema version check with migration scaffolding
description: src/types.ts has version: 1.0 but it is never checked. Add a version guard when reading existing output JSON so future v1.1 breaking changes can route through documented migrations.

title: Phase 3: Per-item <itunes:image> support in RSS generator (infrastructure only)
description: Add plumbing so rss-generator.ts emits <itunes:image href="..."/> inside <item> when Talk.image_url is populated. Leave image_url empty until Phase 4 produces URLs. This decouples the feed-side change from the scrape-side work.

title: Phase 2: HEAD-check audio URLs before emitting them in output JSON
description: Audio URLs extracted by regex are never verified. A URL scheme change (e.g., 128k-lang to 128k_lang) yields invalid mp3 links with no indication. Add optional HEAD verification mode behind a flag.

title: Phase 4: Investigate per-talk imagery on talk pages (beyond speaker portraits)
description: Spike. Determine whether individual talk pages have talk-specific header images or only inherit generic session artwork. If talk-specific art exists, prefer it over speaker portraits.
notes: Spike findings (2026-04-20): BOTTOM LINE: Talk pages DO carry talk-specific imagery - stronger than expected. This should be PRIMARY source, not speaker bio. SAMPLES: 9 talks across 4 conferences (2025-04, 2024-10, 2024-04, 1995-10) - Every talk has unique image hash - Within-session uniqueness confirmed (2025-04 Sat AM Holland/Johnson/Larson all distinct) - URL format: /imgs//full/!,/0/default (IIIF, same CDN as bio portraits) - Hashes are content-addressed, NOT derivable from slug/year/speaker CONTENT CLASSES OBSERVED: - Narrative hero photos (story subjects) - best case (Holland, Andersen) - Referenced artwork (Johnson: 'Five Wise Virgins' painting) - Video poster frames (speaker at pulpit) - procedural talks (Larson, Bednar) - Historical talks (1995) still have distinct per-talk images EXTRACTION SOURCES (need multi-source probe): 1. - present for most modern talks but NOT all 2. window.__INITIAL_STATE__ JSON 'image' field - always present when og:image absent 3. First hero in article body - last resort REVISED PRIORITY for per-item : 1. Talk page og:image (primary) 2. JSON-LD / __INITIAL_STATE__ image (fallback when og missing) 3. First hero img in body (last resort scrape) 4. Speaker bio portrait (rare fallback for talks with no hero) 5. Channel default (final fallback) RISKS: - Double-encoded URLs observed on embedded: /imgs/https%3A%2F%2F...%2Fimgs%2F%2F... - scraper must unwrap - IIIF !768, caps width; podcast needs >=1400px - swap to !1400, or !3000, - Aspect ratio: og:image typically 16:9, Apple wants square - use IIIF region crop or accept letterbox - og:image sometimes hydrated client-side (absent from initial HTML) - __INITIAL_STATE__ fallback mandatory - Opaque hashes: cannot reconstruct offline; must fetch each talk page (but existing cache absorbs cost) - Video-poster talks visually similar across a speaker's talks (procedural items like sustainings/auditing) FEASIBILITY: CONFIRMED, with stronger per-talk value than speaker-bio-only approach.
close_reason: Spike complete. Talk pages carry GENUINELY talk-specific imagery (every talk across 9 samples has unique hash; within-session uniqueness confirmed). This should be the PRIMARY image source, with speaker bio as rare fallback. Extraction needs multi-source probe: og:image -> __INITIAL_STATE__ JSON -> body img. Findings captured in notes; strategy to be synthesized in gc_podcast-gbt.

title: Phase 3: Unit tests for escapeXml edge cases
description: escapeXml at src/rss-generator.ts:79-86 is correct but untested. Add tests for &, <, >, quotes, smart quotes, non-BMP emoji, and ensure outputs are valid XML via a parser.

title: Phase 2: Validate bio URLs with HEAD check, fallback to alternative slug generation
description: src/scraper.ts constructBioUrl() is a slug heuristic that silently produces 404s. Validate via HEAD and try alternative slug strategies (e.g., from meta.title) before storing in JSON.

title: Phase 3: CDATA safety audit - handle content containing ]]>
description: src/rss-generator.ts wraps titles/descriptions in CDATA without splitting on ]]>. Speaker name or talk body containing that sequence would break XML. Add split-and-re-emit logic and a unit test.

title: Phase 2: Cache TTL + explicit hit/miss logging
description: .cache/ grows unbounded and stale HTML masks parser failures. Add configurable TTL (default 7 days) and log cache hit vs miss vs expired per fetch.

title: Phase 3: Ensure monotonic pubDate ordering and document the invariant
description: Current staggering (talk.order * 15 minutes) collides when talks exceed 15 min and Sunday sessions only get +1 day offset. Clients that sort by pubDate can reorder talks. Make pubDate strictly monotonic decreasing within a conference and document ordering guarantee in SPEC.md.

title: Phase 0: Introduce structured logging utility
description: Current code uses console.warn/log with minimal context. Add a small logger module that emits JSON lines with conference/session/talk context, severity, and error codes. Enables later observability work and grep-friendly CI logs.

title: Phase 0: Add CI type-check and lint gate before feed generation
description: .github/workflows/update-feed.yml runs scraping/feed generation but never type-checks or lints. Add 'npx tsc --noEmit' and 'npm run lint' steps before scrape job.

title: Phase 0: Add ESLint + Prettier + pre-commit hook
description: No linting today. Add ESLint (typescript-eslint), Prettier, and husky+lint-staged pre-commit. Keep ruleset minimal; goal is consistency, not style wars.

title: Phase 5: Add baseline security scanning (CodeQL, dependency-review, secret-scanning)
description: Repo has no CodeQL workflow, no dependency-review action on PRs, and relies on GitHub's default secret scanning only. Add a minimal security.yml with CodeQL for JS/TS and dependency-review on pull_request events. Low urgency given the small codebase and no runtime deps, but closes a supply-chain hygiene gap.
close_reason: Created CodeQL and dependency-review workflows with SARIF upload. Added SECURITY.md documenting scanning posture. All actions pinned to SHAs.

title: Phase 6: Document data-format and GUID stability guarantees
description: GUIDs in the RSS feed are stable today (by design), but this is not documented. Add a 'Compatibility Guarantees' section to SPEC.md/README so fork owners and downstream consumers can rely on them.

title: Phase 5: Audit and document workflow permissions
description: contents:write, pages:write, id-token:write are all scoped minimally, but there is no rationale recorded. Add a comment block in update-feed.yml explaining why each permission is required and review whether environment protection rules should gate deploys.
close_reason: Added top-level default permissions (contents: read) and documented per-job overrides with rationale
